info.description: These APIs will help you manage different workflows in Blueshift. Our powerful REST APIs provide an easy way to integrate your data and third party applications with Blueshift.
info.title: Blueshift APIs

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Manage your campaigns in Blueshift.

tags.name: Event
tags.description: Use the event APIs to send events from your servers.

tags.name: Live content
tags.description: Use the Live content API endpoint to insert content recommendations in your website and mobile apps.

tags.name: Email template
tags.description: Email template lifecycle management

tags.name: Push template
tags.description: Push template lifecycle management

tags.name: SMS template
tags.description: SMS template lifecycle management

tags.name: Shared assets
tags.description: Manage reusable assets such as HTML, rich text, subject lines, and visual editor content.

tags.name: Data validation
tags.description: Export data to validate or get counts.

tags.name: Catalog
tags.description: A catalog is a list of items which may include content or products.

tags.name: Customer
tags.description: Blueshift hosts 360 degree customer profile for each of your users to represent all of their demographic, behavioral and engagement activity.

tags.name: Segments
tags.description: A segment is a list of users that satisfy a criteria. For example, you can create a segment for users who are located in the San Francisco area and run campaigns for them.

tags.name: Custom user lists
tags.description: A custom user list contains information about the users of your site such as name, email, phone number, and location. You can use this list as a segment and run campaigns for them.

tags.name: Adapters
tags.description: Adapters are entities that provide integration to various services with Blueshift. For example, we provide adapters for various services such as Mailgun, Sendgrid, and Sparkpost for sending emails, and Infobip and Attentive adapters for SMSes. Similarly, we provide adapters for various channels and integrations.

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: Tags are folder-based entities that you can use to organize your resources. Each tag folder contains its own isolated set of tags.

tags.name: Search
tags.description: You can use the `customer_search` API to search for events associated with a customer.

tags.name: Interest alerts
tags.description: Interest alerts store information about users' interests. When an event occurs in an area or topic in which multiple users are interested, you can trigger a single API call to send a notification to all the users who are interested in that topic.

tags.name: External fetch
tags.description: External fetch is a Blueshift capability that lets you include dynamic content from external servers that is fetched “just in time” before sending a message.

tags.name: Promotions
tags.description: Promotions allows you to manage promo codes that you may wish to send to your customers through Blueshift Campaigns

tags.name: Email validation
tags.description: Validate single or bulk email addresses to assess deliverability and risk.

tags.name: Subscription groups
tags.description: Retrieve subscription groups and view detailed information for each subscription group.

tags.name: Customer groups
tags.description: Customer groups link multiple customer profiles to a common parent entity with shared attributes. Use these endpoints to delete a group or remove a user from a group.

put.description: Use this endpoint to add items to a catalog.

parameters.name: catalog_uuid
parameters.description: Specify the UUID of the catalog to which you want to add the items. You can get the catalog's UUID from its URL. For example, if you open a catalog on the Blueshift app, its URL looks like `https://app.getblueshift.com/dashboard#/app/catalogs/<CATALOG_UUID>/details`.

parameters.name: syncUpdateWithProductData
parameters.description: Optional query parameter. When set to `true`, the API returns the product_id and UUID for products that are added successfully. When set to `false` or not provided, returns a simple status response.

products.description: Specify the items that you want to add to the catalog.

brand.description: Specify the brand name of the item.

category.description: Specify the category identifiers starting from the root of the taxonomy.

image.description: Specify the link to the image of the item. Ensure that the URL that you provide in this field does not contain more than 500 characters.

msrp.description: Specify the maximum suggested retail price of the item (numeric value without currency symbol).

price.description: Specify the selling price of an item (numeric value without currency symbol).

product_id.description: Specify the unique identifier of the item. This is what you pass in the product_ids in your events, and the values specified here must be the same. Ensure that the value that you provide in this field does not contain more than 64 characters.

availability.description: Specify the availability of the item.

tags.description: Specify an array of strings denoting grouping of similar products into collections or groups.

parent_sku.description: Provide the SKU to tie all variants or child products together.

title.description: Specify the title of the item. Ensure that the title that you provide in this field does not contain more than 255 characters.

web_link.description: Specify the URL of the item on your website. Ensure that the URL that you provide in this field does not contain more than 500 characters.

latitude.description: Specify the latitude of the product location (must be between -90 and 90).

longitude.description: Specify the longitude of the product location (must be between -180 and 180).

200.description: Products added successfully. Response format varies based on the `syncUpdateWithProductData` query parameter.

oneOf.description: Response when `syncUpdateWithProductData` is not provided or set to `false`.

product_id.description: The unique identifier of the item that was successfully added.

uuid.description: The UUID of the item in Blueshift.

failure.description: Array of items that failed to be added.

product.description: The product details that failed validation.

without_sync_param.summary: Response without syncUpdateWithProductData or when set to false

put.description: Use this endpoint to add a user to a custom list.

parameters.name: list_id
parameters.description: Specify the ID of the custom user list to which you want to add a user. You can find the list ID in the URL when viewing the list in the Blueshift app. For example: `https://app.getblueshift.com/dashboard#/app/custom_list/41148/edit` - the list ID here is `41148`.

identifier_key.description:
Specify how you want to identify the user:

• Use `email` to identify by email address
• Use `customer_id` to identify by customer ID

This must match the source type configured when the list was created.

identifier_value.description:
Specify the actual identifier value:

• If `identifier_key` is `email`: provide the user's email address (e.g., `john.doe@example.com`)
• If `identifier_key` is `customer_id`: provide the customer ID (e.g., `23a1fae1-2103-4e18-b58c-9db0ea9f7bf7`)

You can find the customer ID in the URL when viewing a customer profile: `https://app.getblueshift.com/dashboard#/app/customer/<CUSTOMER_ID>/show/overview`

parameters.name: segment_uuid
parameters.description: UUID of the segment.

parameters.name: refresh
parameters.description: Trigger job to update segment counts. Counts are updated asynchronously in the background. Use refresh=true to trigger a new count update, and subsequently poll with refresh=false to retrieve updated counts.

parameters.name: channels
parameters.description: Channels can be one of: 'users', 'email', 'push', 'sms', or 'inApp'. Parameter indicates which channel audience counts to refresh.

parameters.name: bypass_global
parameters.description: Set 'true' to return counts bypassing the global inclusion segments.

refresh_status.description:
'refreshing'- indicates count calculation job has triggered and awaiting counts to be computed in the background.

'ready' - indicates count calculation job completed and counts have been updated at timestamp indicated in 'approxusers_updated_at'.

approxusers_updated_at.description: Timestamp when segment counts were last updated.

post.description: Use this endpoint to send an event from your server or use javascript events to call the API.

customer_id.description: Specify the customer ID. You must specify information in either this field or atleast in one of the following fields: `event`, `device_id`, `email`, or `group_id`. These fields are described below.

event.description:
Specify the name of the event. For example identify, view (product view), add_to_cart, remove_from_cart, checkout, purchase, search, your_custom_event_name (custom event) etc. You must atleast specify this value, or one or more of the following fields: customer_id/device_id/email. 
 > **📘** Ensure that the event name does not contain periods (`.`), whitespaces, is not a numeric value, and contains not more than 64 characters.

device_type.description: Specify the type of the device.

device_token.description:
Specify the token of the device.
 > **📘** If you use the event API to identify a user from a mobile app, ensure that you also include `device_id` and `device_token` parameters in your API call. However, we recommend that you use our SDK to identify users on a mobile app. For more information, see [Track events on your Android app](https://developer.blueshift.com/docs/events-tracking-android-sdk) and [Track events on your iOS app](https://developer.blueshift.com/docs/track-events-on-your-app-with-blueshifts-ios-sdk).

device_id.description:
Specify the UUID of the device. You must atleast specify this value, or one or more of the following fields: event/customer_id/email.
 > **📘** If you use the event API to identify a user from a mobile app, ensure that you also include `device_id` and `device_token` parameters in your API call.  However, we recommend that you use our SDK to identify users on a mobile app. For more information, see [Track events on your Android app](https://developer.blueshift.com/docs/events-tracking-android-sdk) and [Track events on your iOS app](https://developer.blueshift.com/docs/track-events-on-your-app-with-blueshifts-ios-sdk).

device_idfa.description: Sepcify the advertising identifier (idfa) on the device.

device_idfv.description: Specify the identfier for vendor (idfv) on the device.

device_manufacturer.description: Specify the manufacturer of the device.

network_carrier.description: Specify the carrier on the mobile device.

ip.description: Specify the IP address of the device.

email.description: Specify the user's email address. For example, abc@def.com. You must atleast specify this value, or one or more of the following fields: event/device_id/customer_id.

latitude.description: Specify the latitude of the user's location.

longitude.description: Specify the longitude of the user's location.

event_uuid.description: Specify a unique identifier of the event in the format `1234abcd-efghijkj-1234kfjadslk-34iu123`. For reference on the UUID format, see [RFC 4122](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4122.html). Ensure that the `event_uuid` that you specify is unique. This is an optional field and you can leave this field blank.

cookie.description: User's Blueshift cookie used as an identifier for anonymous users.

id.description: The ID of the subscription group.

subscribed.description: Enter `true` if the user is subscribed, or `false` if unsubscribed.

requestBody.description: Specify device_id. The device_id is the mobile specific (iOS or Android) device identifier to uniquely identify the mobile device.

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a campaign and specify its attributes.

parameters.name: campaign_type
parameters.description: Specify how the campaign is triggered. Choose an option from the list.

name.description: The name of the campaign.

author.description: Email address of the campaign author.

launch.description:
Determines if the campaign should be launched immediately (`true`) or saved as a draft (`false`). 

**Default**: `true`.

startdate.description:
Campaign start time in ISO 8601 format. 

**Default**: current time.

enddate.description:
Campaign end time in ISO 8601 format. 

**Default**: Runs indefinitely. 

**Applicable only for event-triggered campaigns.**

segment_uuid.description:
UUID of the segment associated with this campaign. 

**Required only for one-time campaigns.**

triggering_event_name.description:
The event that triggers this campaign. Must match an **Event Attribute** in the Events dashboard.  

If left blank, the campaign is **API triggered** and can only be triggered via the **Campaign Execute API**. 

**Applicable only for event-triggered campaigns.**

bypass_global_inclusion_segment.description:
Whether to send messages to users outside the global inclusion segment. 

**Default**: `false`.

skip_incrementing_user_message_limits.description:
Controls whether sent messages are excluded from the user’s message counts. 

When set to `true`, messages do not count towards the user’s message limits. 

**Default**: `false`.

remove_unsubscribe_from_email_headers.description:
Whether to exclude unsubscribe headers from emails. 

**Default**: `false`.

send_summary_emails.description:
Email address to receive the campaign execution summary. 

**Applicable only for one-time campaigns.**

send_to_unsubscribed.description:
Whether to send messages to unsubscribed users. 

**Default**: `false`.

journey_concurrency.description:
Defines how often a user can enter the journey. 

**Options:** `once_per_lifetime`, `once_at_any_time`, `multiple`. 

**Default**: `multiple`.

seed_list_uuids.description: An array of seed list UUIDs for testing campaigns.

tag_data.description:
Tags associated with the campaign. 

**Format**: `folder_name: tag1, tag2, tag3`. 

**Example**: `Regions:USA,Canada|Products:Electronics,Apparel`.

triggers.description: You can add only one trigger at a time. If you add multiple triggers, the endpoint returns an error.

trigger_name.description: Name of the trigger for reference.

template_uuid.description: UUID of the template linked to this trigger (email, push, SMS, etc.).

utm_source.description:
UTM Source for tracking. 

**Default**: Set based on the account's backend configuration.

utm_campaign.description:
UTM Campaign name. 

**Default**: Set based on the account's backend configuration.

utm_medium.description:
UTM Medium (e.g., email, push). 

**Default**: Set based on the account's backend configuration.

utm_content.description:
Identifies specific content variations within the campaign. 

**Default**: Set based on the account's backend configuration.

utm_term.description:
UTM Term for keyword tracking. 

**Default**: Set based on the account's backend configuration.

account_adapter_uuid.description:
UUID of the adapter for message sending. If left blank, the trigger uses the default adapter for the channel. 

Not applicable for cloud app triggers. 

**Leave blank if using `account_adapter_liquid_expression`**.

account_adapter_liquid_expression.description:
Liquid expression to dynamically assign an adapter UUID. 

**Leave `account_adapter_uuid` blank when using this**.

from_address.description: Sender's email address. It must match the adapter domain.

reply_to_address.description: Email address where replies are sent.

custom_url_params.description: Custom tracking parameters added to links.

name.description: Specify the name of the property.

value.description: Specify the value of the property.

requestBody.description: Specifications for the campaign to be created.

uuid.description: The unique ID of the created campaign.

name.description: The name of the created campaign.

startdate.description: The date and time when the campaign starts in ISO 8601 format.

enddate.description: The date and time when the campaign ends in ISO 8601 format.

status.description: The current status of the campaign.

triggering_event_name.description: Specify the event that triggers this campaign.

journey_concurrency.description: The number of times a campaign runs for a user.

parameters.description: Search for email templates that contain the specified string in the name of the template.

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Search for templates with specified archived status. Specify value as `0` to get a list of un-archived templates, or as `1` to get a list of archived templates. Leave blank to get a list of all templates.

parameters.name: resource.editor_type
parameters.description: Search for templates of the specified type. Specify value as `html` to get a list of HTML templates or `bee_editor` to get a list of Visual Editor templates. Leave blank to get a list of all templates.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Specify the number of records to be returned per page

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Specify the page number for the search results.

post.description: Use this endpoint to send a test mail to a specific email address.

id.description: Specify the UUID of the template. The template uuids can be found by [querying the index](https://developer.blueshift.com/reference/email-template#get_api-v1-email-templates-json), or from the URL when you view a template in the Blueshift app. For example, in `https://app.getblueshift.com/dashboard#/email_template_studio/<TEMPLATE_UUID>/edit/info`, the `<TEMPLATE_UUID>` is the uuid you need.

personalize_for.description: Specify the email address of the user whose personalization context we should use to send the test email.

recipients.description: Specify email addresses of the recipients

from_name.description:
Specify the name that we should use in the `from` field of the email.
 > **📘** This is only applicable for the email channel.

from_address.description:
Specify the email address that we should use as the sender's email address. For example, `support@blueshift.com`.
 > **📘** This is only applicable for the email channel. 
You can take a look at documentation on [SendGrid](https://help.blueshift.com/hc/en-us/articles/360046787053-SendGrid-Email#h_6da2d422-23a0-4504-b0ce-000b203201fd) for reference. If you use a different adapter, you can review its documentation too.

reply_to_address.description:
Specify the email address to which your customers can reply to, if they want to reply to an email that our platform sends.
 > **📘** This is only applicable for the email channel. 
You can take a look at documentation on [SendGrid](https://help.blueshift.com/hc/en-us/articles/360046787053-SendGrid-Email#h_6da2d422-23a0-4504-b0ce-000b203201fd) for reference. If you use a different adapter, you can review its documentation too.

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a shared asset.

display_name.description: Human-readable name for the asset. Used to generate the shareable_name if not provided.

content.description: The actual content of the asset. For HTML assets, provide HTML markup. For Subject assets, provide plain text.

asset_type.description: Type of asset to create. Must be either 'html' or 'subject'. Cannot be changed after creation.

plain_text.description: Plain text version of the content. Only applicable for HTML assets. If not provided, Blueshift will auto-generate it.

is_plain_text_custom.description: Set to true if providing a custom plain_text version, false to auto-generate. Only applicable for HTML assets.

author.description: Name of the user who created the asset.

email.description: Email of the user who created the asset.

post.description: Triggers a launched S3 customer attributes import task.

uuid.description: The UUID of the customer attributes import task. You can find the UUID in the import task URL on the Blueshift dashboard. For example, for the URL `https://app.getblueshift.com/dashboard#/app/customer/import_show/23114f03-5462-4de5-b112-d316e02650d1`, the UUID is `23114f03-5462-4de5-b112-d316e02650d1`.

requestBody.description: Provide the UUID of the customer attributes import task that you want to trigger.

405.description: Method not allowed - The import cannot be triggered. This can occur if the import source is not Amazon S3, the import is not in launched state, or the import is already being processed.

delete.description: Deletes a customer group. Set `delete_users` to keep or remove the associated users.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: The ID of the customer group to delete. This is not the UUID.

parameters.name: delete_users
parameters.description:
Determines whether the users associated with the group are also deleted.

- `false` — Deletes the group and preserves all associated user records.
- `true` — Deletes the group and permanently removes all associated users.

200.description: OK — The group was deleted successfully.

delete_users.summary: Group and associated users deleted (delete_users=true or omitted)
